title: Junior Real Estate Bookkeeper (Remote – Philippines / Overseas VA)
description:
Third Little Pig Bookkeeping

Junior Real Estate Bookkeeper (Remote – Philippines / Overseas VA)

Part-Time | 10–15 hrs/week to start (15–20 as client load grows) | $10–$15/hr USD (experience-based) | Flexible Hours (Must overlap 9am–11pm EST)



About Third Little Pig Bookkeeping

Third Little Pig Bookkeeping exists to bring clarity and structural integrity to the financial lives of small business owners — from real estate investors and contractors to e-commerce entrepreneurs and service-based businesses. Founded in 2017 by Sal Sannuto, the firm was built on a simple belief: most bookkeeping is a mess, and it doesn't have to be.



Sal brings a rare combination of hands-on business experience — in real estate investment, e-commerce, and small business ownership — and meticulous attention to detail that most bookkeepers simply don't have. When you work with Third Little Pig, you're not getting a number-cruncher. You're getting a real person who understands your business, responds quickly, and actually cares whether your books are right.



We build the brick house — stable, accurate, and built to last.



We live by our values:

Integrity — We don't oversell, overpromise, or cut corners. What we say is what we do.

Accuracy — Clean reconciliations, proper classification, and structured documentation. The details matter because your decisions depend on them.

Responsiveness — Direct communication, fast replies, no ticket queues. Clients work with a real person who knows their business.

Simplicity — Standardized systems and repeatable workflows mean consistent results.



The Role

We are looking for a detail-oriented, self-directed Junior Real Estate Bookkeeper to join our growing remote team. This is a part-time contractor position starting at approximately 10–15 hours per week, growing to 15–20 hours as you get up to speed — with clear potential to become full-time as our client base expands.



You will work directly under the owner/founder, handling the day-to-day bookkeeping tasks for a portfolio of real estate investor clients — primarily using Xero — so the owner can focus on client relationships, business development, and strategic growth.



This is NOT just a task-taking role. We want someone who takes ownership, communicates proactively, and helps build a more efficient practice over time.



Core Responsibilities

Bookkeeping & Financial Accuracy

Record and categorize all transactions weekly for real estate investor clients (primarily cash-basis, with some accrual-basis clients due to invoicing).

Reconcile bank and credit card accounts across multiple clients and entities.

Track revenue and expenses by property, project, or portfolio.

Prepare and maintain accurate monthly financial reports and flag any discrepancies or concerns.

Handle fixed asset entries where applicable, including depreciation and project-specific CapEx tracking.

Review tax returns to validate reporting accuracy and support year-end preparation.

Manage installment sale reconciliation when relevant (knowledge in this area is a plus).

Client Portal & Communication Management (via Double HQ)

Log in to Double HQ daily and prioritize clearing the three key queues: (1) outstanding client questions / inbound queries, (2) pending client uploads, and (3) transaction questions awaiting client response.

Respond to client portal activity promptly — inbound client questions should be addressed the same day.

Escalate unresolved items or non-responsive clients to the owner in a timely manner with a clear log of outreach attempts.

Keep client files and task statuses updated and accurate in Double HQ at all times.



Systems & SOPs

Follow and maintain existing Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for financial workflows.

Suggest improvements to current accounting processes and documentation when you identify a better way.

Assist in building and refining client-specific templates within Double HQ as part of onboarding new accounts.



Communication & Collaboration

Communicate clearly and proactively with the owner via Slack — flag concerns early, don't wait.

Participate in weekly check-ins to review client statuses, outstanding items, and any escalations.

Complete Double HQ Academy training within your first week on the job.

Be a thought partner, not just a task-taker — if something doesn't look right, say so.



Tools We Use (or You'll Learn)

Xero (primary accounting software — proficiency required)

QuickBooks Online (a strong plus — we occasionally onboard QBO clients)

Double HQ (client dashboard and workflow management — training provided; complete Double HQ Academy in Week 1)

Google Workspace — Gmail, Google Drive, Google Sheets, Google Calendar, Google Meet

Slack (primary team communication)

LastPass (password management)

Loom (asynchronous video training and updates)

Gusto (payroll — at least one client uses this)





You're a Perfect Fit If…

You have at least 5+ years of professional bookkeeping experience, with at least 2 years specifically in real estate bookkeeping.

You are proficient in Xero — this is non-negotiable. QuickBooks Online experience is a strong bonus.

You understand cash-basis accounting for real estate investors and can manage multiple client entities cleanly.

You are comfortable with real estate-specific transactions: rental income, property purchases and sales, rehab costs, refinances, and depreciation entries.

You have knowledge of installment sale reconciliation (a significant plus for our client base).

You can confidently spot discrepancies, follow up on missing information, and produce clean monthly financials.

You are an excellent written and verbal English communicator — clear, kind, and professional. Strong English fluency is a firm requirement for this role.

You're highly organized and can manage multiple client accounts and deadlines without dropping the ball.

You build and improve bookkeeping systems — not just follow them.

You are comfortable working independently in a remote, async-first environment with a small, growing team.

You are a self-starter who takes ownership of your work and communicates proactively — not reactively.



Qualifications

5+ years of professional bookkeeping experience.

2+ years of bookkeeping experience specifically with real estate investors or real estate-adjacent businesses.

Xero proficiency (required). QuickBooks Online experience (strong plus).

Strong knowledge of GAAP and real estate bookkeeping best practices.

Xero Advisor Certification or equivalent is a plus.

Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills.

Strong English fluency (written and verbal) — this is a firm requirement.

Experience with property-level tracking, cash-basis bookkeeping, and multi-entity management.







Why Work With Us

Fully remote — work from anywhere (Philippines or overseas).

Flexible hours within a wide availability window (9am–11pm EST). We don't micromanage your schedule — we care about results.

Opportunity to grow with us — this role starts part-time and is designed to grow into a full-time position as our client base expands.

Clear SOPs, organized systems, and a supportive owner who is invested in your success and onboarding.

Weekly team check-ins to keep you connected, supported, and aligned.

Work with a focused, real estate-specialized bookkeeping firm — not a generalist shop.

Hourly rate based on experience: $10/hr for candidates with 1–3 years of real estate bookkeeping experience; $12–$15/hr for candidates with 5+ years and Xero certification.





Schedule & Availability

This is a part-time contractor role, approximately 10–15 hours per week to start, growing to 15–20 hours as you become familiar with clients and workflows.



Work hours are flexible within a wide window: 9:00am – 11:00pm Eastern Standard Time (EST).

You do not need to work exclusively during US business hours — most bookkeeping work is async and deadline-driven.

You are expected to be reachable via Slack and able to respond to client portal activity within the agreed same-day window.

You must be available for a weekly check-in meeting (time to be agreed upon at hire).



Note for Philippines-based applicants: Eastern Time is typically 12 hours behind Philippine Standard Time — evening/night hours PST are perfectly fine and expected.





How to Apply

We review every application carefully. We're looking for quality over speed - take your time and show us who you are.

Please submit your application here: 
 



What to expect in the application:

Written responses to questions - answer in your own words. We will check for AI-generated responses.

We're evaluating how you communicate, how you think, and whether you're the right fit for our team.

Shortlisted applicants will be invited to a video interview.



Timeline

Applications open until: Position filled

Interviews will be conducted: Beginning first week of April

Target start date: April 15, 2026

title: Affiliate Marketing Manager (CJ Platform)
description:
Overview

We’re looking for a performance-driven Affiliate Marketing Manager to own and scale our affiliate channel through CJ (Commission Junction). This role is responsible for driving revenue growth, building high-quality publisher relationships, and optimizing program performance across content, loyalty, and deal partners.



You’re not just managing a program—you’re actively growing it. Please apply through this link: 



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Program Ownership & Growth

- Own day-to-day management of the CJ affiliate program

- Develop and execute strategies to drive scalable revenue growth

- Set and hit monthly/quarterly performance targets (revenue, ROAS, CPA)



Publisher Recruitment & Relationship Management

- Proactively recruit new publishers across content, commerce, loyalty, and influencer verticals

- Build and maintain strong relationships with top-performing affiliates

- Negotiate placements, commissions, and exclusive opportunities



Campaign & Offer Management

- Plan and execute promotional calendars (seasonal, product launches, tentpole 
nts)

- Create compelling offers, bonuses, and incentives to drive partner performance

- Collaborate with internal teams on product priorities and messaging



Performance Optimization

- Analyze affiliate performance data and identify growth opportunities

- Continuously optimize commission structures, partner mix, and placements

- Monitor attribution, incrementality, and partner quality



Platform Management (CJ)

- Manage CJ dashboard, including partner approvals, link tracking, creatives, and reporting

- Ensure proper tracking, attribution, and compliance across all partners

- Troubleshoot technical or tracking issues as they arise



Cross-Functional Collaboration

- Work with paid media, influencer, and content teams to align strategies

- Partner with creative teams to develop high-performing affiliate assets

- Coordinate with finance on payouts, budgets, and forecasting



Qualifications

3+ years of affiliate marketing experience, with hands-on CJ platform expertise

Proven track record of driving revenue growth through affiliate programs

Strong relationships across affiliate publishers (media, content, loyalty, etc.)

Deep understanding of performance marketing metrics (CPA, ROAS, LTV)

Strong analytical and reporting skills

Experience negotiating partnerships and placements

Highly proactive—comfortable building from scratch, not just maintaining



Nice to Have

Experience working with DTC brands (especially in CPG, beauty, or apparel)

Familiarity with other affiliate platforms (Impact, Rakuten, ShareASale)

Understanding of influencer/creator affiliate models

Experience with promo strategy and retail calendars



What Success Looks Like

Consistent MoM revenue growth from the affiliate channel

Expansion of high-quality, incremental publisher partnerships

Efficient CPA/ROAS aligned with broader performance goals

A program that feels actively managed—not passive

title: General Virtual Assistant
description:
We are looking for a trustworthy and self reliant Entry Level Virtual Assistant / Admin Virtual Assistant to support our business on an hourly, as needed basis.



This is a good opportunity for someone who is dependable, resourceful, willing to learn, and able to work independently once given instructions. Entry level applicants are very welcome to apply.



This role is remote, hourly, and per need basis. Please note that being hired for this position does not mean you will automatically begin billing regular or full time hours. Work will only be assigned based on actual business needs. Some days or weeks may have more tasks, while others may have fewer.



You will be working inside ClickUp, so you must be comfortable using it or be willing to learn quickly.



Rate: $2 per hour



Work Setup:

Remote

Hourly

As needed / per need basis

Must be available during Eastern Time working hours



Important Availability Expectation:

You must be actively available during Eastern Time working hours. This means that when tasks, updates, or instructions are given, we may expect a reply within 1 to 2 hours during the workday. We are looking for someone who is already working and accessible during Eastern hours, not someone who is only occasionally checking messages.



What You’ll Be Doing:

-General administrative support

-Internet research

-Data entry

-Organizing files and information

-Updating task statuses in ClickUp

-Following up on assigned tasks

-Basic documentation and admin support

-Other virtual assistant tasks as needed



What We’re Looking For:

-Trustworthy

-Self reliant

-Resourceful

-Detail oriented

-Open to learning

-Able to follow instructions well

-Willing to be trained

-Responsive during working hours

-Comfortable working independently once given direction



**A big part of this role is being able to take a task, research what is needed, organize the information properly, and move things forward without needing constant follow up.

**We are also looking for someone who is open to learning how to use AI tools to improve speed, quality, and efficiency. You do not need to be an expert already, but you must be willing to learn and apply new tools.



Requirements:

-Must be available during Eastern Time working hours

-Must be willing to work inside ClickUp

-Must have a valid TIN

-Must be registered with the BIR as a freelancer

-Must be able to provide a Service Invoice



Who This Role Is Best For:

-This role is ideal for someone who wants to gain experience, build skills, and grow in a work environment that values reliability, initiative, and learning.



If you are honest, dependable, responsive, and willing to learn, we would like to hear from you.



How to Apply:

1. Please complete our Google Form here:


 

Only applicants who complete the form will be considered.



2. Once done, please reply to this job post.

title: AI Music Producer
description:
What you'll do

You'll use AI music generation tools (Suno, Udio, or similar) to create original tracks based on reference playlists and creative briefs provided by our team. You'll be responsible for:



--- Analyzing reference playlists to identify genre, tempo, mood, instrumentation, and sonic characteristics



--- Crafting detailed AI prompts to generate music that matches the vibe and quality of the reference material



--- Iterating on outputs — selecting the best generations, refining prompts, and delivering polished tracks



--- Organizing and labeling output files clearly (genre, BPM, mood tags) for easy library management



--- Hitting track volume targets — we expect a consistent daily/weekly output quota



--- Staying current with new AI music tools and techniques, and flagging any that could improve our workflow



What we're looking for

--- Strong ear for music — you can identify what makes a track work: groove, energy, vibe, and production style



--- Hands-on experience with AI music tools such as Suno, Udio, Stable Audio, or similar platforms



--- Ability to translate a playlist or brief into clear, effective generation prompts



--- Self-directed, organized, and able to manage a high-volume creative workflow independently



--- Background in music production, audio engineering, or music theory is a strong plus — but not required



--- Familiarity with music genres relevant to streaming catalogs (hip-hop, R&B, pop, electronic, deep house)



--- Reliable internet connection and ability to work independently during overlap hours with the US



Bonus points if you have

--- Experience distributing AI-generated music to DSPs (Spotify, Apple Music, etc.)



--- Knowledge of metadata, PRO registration, or catalog tagging for streaming



--- Light audio editing skills (trimming, mastering, format conversion) in tools like Audacity or Adobe Audition



How to apply

To be considered, please include the following in your application message:



1. A short intro — who you are and your background with music and AI tools



2. Samples — 2–3 tracks you've created using AI music tools (Soundcloud, Google Drive, or similar links)



3. Your process — briefly describe how you'd approach a reference playlist to generate a batch of tracks



4. Tools you use — list the AI music platforms you're most comfortable with

title: Google Ads Manager
description:
We’re looking for an experienced Google Ads Manager to support a new done-for-you service inside our business.

A couple of our Boardroom clients are in real need of strong Google Ads support, so we want to bring in someone who can manage ads for them well, get results, and make this an easy, high-value service for the client.



This is not an hourly role.



You’ll be paid a project rate per client, depending on your experience, the quality of your portfolio, and the kind of results you’ve been able to achieve.



What you’ll be doing

Each scholarship recipient receives:

Setting up and managing Google Ads campaigns for clients

Doing keyword research and audience targeting

Writing ad copy that converts

Monitoring performance and making regular optimisations

Improving lead quality, conversions, and return on ad spend

Tracking results and reporting clearly

title: Cold Calling + Email Sales Rep (Hourly + Commission)
description:
We’re a fast-growing outbound team working with small agency clients, helping them turn positive cold
 replies into booked calls and real sales opportunities.



This is not a generic admin role.



We are looking for someone who is confident in sales, excellent in English, highly organised, and fast when it comes to handling lead conversations.



In this role, you’ll be managing conversations with warm prospects who have replied to cold
 campaigns. Your job is to reply quickly, handle the conversation well, and move the prospect toward booking a call with the salesperson inside the client’s business.



Some campaigns will also require calling warm prospects who have already replied positively, so this role is ideal for someone who is strong both in written sales and on the phone.



You’ll typically be working across 5 to 15 client campaigns at a time, so being sharp, organised, and dependable matters.



What You’ll Be Doing:



Managing inboxes for multiple cold
 campaigns



Replying quickly to positive and interested leads



Turning warm conversations into booked calls



Following scripts, processes, and booking flows



Calling warm prospects for selected client campaigns



Keeping lead conversations organised across multiple accounts



Supporting campaign performance by handling replies well and consistently



Working closely with our team across different client accounts



What Makes This Role Different:



You are speaking with warm prospects, not completely cold leads



The main goal is to create booked calls, not just send messages



You’ll be involved in both written sales and phone-based follow-up



You’ll be working across multiple live campaigns, not just one account



There is room to grow if you perform well



Pay Structure:



$3.75 USD per hour from our agency



Additional earning opportunities through one of our partner client campaigns



Small commission / bonus per booked call on specific calling campaigns



Strong performers can materially increase their earnings through incentives



This is a blended role. You’ll be part of our team, but for some calling work you may also support a specific partner client campaign that we are involved in. We will train you, manage you, and support you directly.



Who We’re Looking For:

Required:



Strong written and spoken English



Confident sales communication skills



Fast reply speed and strong attention to detail



Comfortable speaking with prospects on the phone



Ability to manage multiple conversations and campaigns at once



Reliable internet and a professional remote work setup



Hungry, dependable, and performance-driven



Strong Plus:



Cold calling experience



Appointment setting experience



Inbox management experience



Experience working with cold
 campaigns



Experience using CRMs, dialers, or sales tools



Who This Role Suits:

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys sales, likes moving conversations forward, and wants more than a basic remote job.



If you are sharp, organised, persuasive, and motivated by performance, this could be a strong fit.



How to Apply:

To apply, complete this Google Form:





As part of your application, you must create a personalised Loom video and submit the Loom link inside the Google Form.



In your Loom video, tell us:



What sales experience you have



Why you are interested in a remote role focused on cold
 and sales



Why you think you would be a good fit for this role



Please keep your video to 2 minutes or less.



Applications without a Loom video will not be considered.

title: Digital Ads Manager (Google & Meta) – Execution-Focused Role
description:
Digital Ads Manager (Google & Meta) – Execution-Focused Role

About the Role



We are a growing digital marketing agency focused on helping small and mid-sized businesses generate consistent, measurable leads.



As we continue to expand into 2026, paid advertising plays an increasingly important role in how we deliver results for our clients. We are looking for a Digital Ads Manager who enjoys hands-on work inside ad platforms — someone who is detail-oriented, analytical, and motivated by improving performance over time.



This is an execution-focused position. You will not be responsible for client communication, sales, or building landing pages. Instead, your role is to manage and improve paid advertising performance while working alongside a team that supports strategy, copywriting, and conversion optimization.



Mission of the Role

Core Responsibilities



Plan, launch, and manage paid campaigns across Google Ads and Meta Ads



Monitor budgets, bids, and pacing to ensure campaigns stay on track



Continuously test audiences, keywords, creatives, and ad formats

Familiar with GA4 (Google Analytics 4)



Basic understanding of Google Tag Manager

Understanding of attribution differences between platforms



Comfortable reviewing lead quality data (with team support)

What Success Looks Like



Campaigns are well-structured and organized



Budgets are managed and paced correctly



Performance improves through consistent testing and optimization



Tracking issues are identified early



Insights are clearly communicated to the team



You work independently while collaborating effectively



You proactively identify opportunities and potential problems



Nice to Have (Not Required)

Why Join Us

Our Core Values

title: IRS Tax Resolution Specialist
description:
**ONLY APPLY IF YOU HAVE 2 YEARS EXPERIENCE ONLY**



Role Overview:



We are seeking a highly organized and experienced Tax Resolution Virtual Assistant based in the Philippines to support our growing tax practice. The ideal candidate has a deep understanding of IRS collection processes and can independently manage tax resolution cases from start to finish.



Key Responsibilities:

Case Management: Prepare and file IRS/State forms, including Power of Attorney (2848), Offer in Compromise (656), and Collection Information Statements (433-A/B).



Transcript Analysis: Pull and analyze IRS transcripts to determine CSEDs (Collection Statute Expiration Dates) and identify resolution options.



Resolution Strategy: Assist in evaluating clients for Installment Agreements, Currently Not Collectible (CNC) status, and Penalty Abatements.



Communication: Liaison between the firm and the IRS/State authorities; follow up on pending cases and request stays of collection.



Documentation: Organize financial records, bank statements, and proof of expenses for case substantiation.



Requirements

Experience: At least 2+ years of direct experience in US Tax Resolution.

Software: Proficiency in tax resolution software (e.g., PitBullTax, Canopy, or TaxHelp) and CRM tools.

Knowledge: Firm grasp of IRS collection standards and the Internal Revenue Manual (IRM).

Skills: Exceptional written and verbal English; extreme attention to detail.

Credentials: Enrolled Agent (EA) or CPA status is a major plus but not required.



Work Schedule & Compensation

Hours: [Insert hours, e.g., 9 PM – 5 AM PHT / US EST Business Hours]

Rate: Competitive hourly rate based on experience.

Benefits: Philippine holidays observed, and performance bonuses.

mber who keeps missing deadlines? Give us a real example.

title: Growth and Sales Manager
description:
About You 

You want to be part of something with massive potential, there from the ground floor. 

You thrive in environments where the expectations are high, the goal is challenging, the mission is clear, and the team supports each other to get better every day. 

You’re looking for a role with real responsibility, where you feel ownership over what you build. 

You are excited by working directly with a successful entrepreneur who invests in you. 

You want to build something that supports others through stable, dignified, growth-oriented jobs. 

You are a natural closer with a strategist’s mind — equally energized by understanding what makes people say “yes” and building the repeatable systems that turn those yeses into scale. 



Role Overview 

The Growth & Sales Manager is the engine behind Moonlight’s client acquisition strategy. As the Growth & Sales Manager you have a deep instinct for what drives people to say “yes” and the systems-thinking discipline to scale that into a repeatable process. You have a creative and entrepreneurial mind. You are adept at all lead gen tactics — whether it’s cold
, partnerships, webinars, LinkedIn plays, or unconventional outreach strategies. You will work closely with our Digital Marketer to bridge brand and performance — ensuring that outbound efforts are aligned with the broader marketing strategy and that Moonlight is continually being introduced to new audiences in compelling ways. 

Equal parts closer and strategist, this role is responsible for building, refining, and executing our entire outbound and inbound sales funnel — from lead generation through to conversion, onboarding, and renewal. This person is relentlessly outcome-driven, deeply persuasive, and thrives in a fast-paced, early-stage environment where growth is the mandate.



Qualifications 

? 5-7+ years of experience in a sales, growth, or business development role, ideally in a fast-paced startup or service-based business 

? Proven track record of exceeding sales targets and closing high-value clients through outbound channels 

? Deep understanding of sales psychology, lead qualification, and consultative selling 

? Experience designing or optimizing a high-converting sales funnel — from cold outreach to contract 

? Strong proficiency with CRM systems (e.g., HubSpot, Pipedrive) and sales automation tools ? Excellent verbal and written communication skills — persuasive, clear, and human 

? Highly analytical and data-driven with the ability to identify drop-off points and improve conversion rates 

 Creative thinker who enjoys testing and iterating on outreach strategies, messaging, and formats

? Comfortable collaborating with marketers, operators, and leadership to align growth across functions 

? Thrives in a lean, early-stage environment with high autonomy and a bias toward action 

? Excellent project management and organizational abilities; adept at balancing multiple priorities under tight timelines. 

? Comfort and skill in working remotely with distributed, multicultural teams 

Compensation, Growth, and Benefits 

? Competitive compensation commensurate with experience 

? Opportunity for performance-based bonuses as the company grows 

? Direct mentorship from the founder and CEO 

? 21 days of paid time off 

? Health benefits (in progress) 

? Philippines 13th month end of year bonus 



Apply here:

title: Junior Marketing Manager | Remote, Full-time
description:
WE ARE INTERVIEWING THIS FRIDAY (20/03). Apply now if you want to be considered.



Have you ever been the person who keeps a marketing team organized and on track? The one who makes sure nothing falls through the cracks? If that sounds like you, keep reading.



We help creators sell high-ticket online programs. We build their funnels, run their ads, write their copy, and manage everything behind the scenes. We have a team of specialists who each handle their part — a media buyer, copywriters, a funnel builder, and ops people.



What we need is someone to keep all of these people coordinated.



You'll be the person in the middle who makes sure the right things get done by the right people at the right time. When we're launching a campaign for a client, you make sure every piece is ready before it goes live. You follow up, you check the details, you keep things moving.



You don't need to be an expert in ads, copywriting, or funnel building. You need to be the kind of person who can manage a team, stay on top of deadlines, and make sure nothing gets missed.



WHAT YOUR DAY LOOKS LIKE



Check in with tea
mbers to make sure tasks are on track — the media buyer is setting up ads, the copywriter has delivered the
s, the ops person has loaded everything into GoHighLevel



Before a campaign goes live, go through a checklist: Is the funnel built? Is the copy loaded? Are the automations working? Are the ads approved? Is tracking in place?



Keep a clear schedule of what's happening across all clients — who is launching when, what deadlines are coming up, what needs attention



When something is running behind or a problem comes up, you flag it right away and help the team figure out the solution



Create simple SOPs (step-by-step instructions) so the team does things the same way every time. If we do something more than once, there should be a process for it.



Send weekly updates to the founding partners — what launched, what's coming up, any issues to be aware of

Keep our tools and subscriptions organized. If we're paying for something we don't use, you catch it.



WHO WILL DO WELL IN THIS ROLE



You've coordinated a team before. Maybe you were an operations manager, a project manager, a team lead, or a marketing coordinator at an agency or online business. You know what it's like to keep multiple people and projects moving at the same time.



You're organized and detail-oriented. You use checklists, spreadsheets, trackers — whatever it takes to make sure nothing gets missed.



You communicate clearly and proactively. If something is going to be late, you don't wait until the deadline passes. You speak up early.



You're comfortable learning new tools. Our main tools are GoHighLevel, Notion, Zapier/Make, Google Workspace, and Zoom. You don't need to be an expert in all of them right now, but you should be comfortable picking them up.



You've worked in digital marketing, online businesses, or the creator/coaching space before. You understand what a funnel is, what a webinar launch involves, and how online marketing campaigns work — even if you haven't done these things yourself.

You're reliable. When you say something will get done, it gets done.



If you've been a VA who has grown into managing projects and teams, or you've been a marketing operations person at an agency — this could be a great fit. We care more about how you think and work than about your job title.



WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT



You won't be writing copy, running ads, or building funnels. You're managing the people who do those things.

This is not a basic VA role where you just follow instructions. You'll need to think ahead, solve problems, and take ownership.

You don't need to be a marketing genius. But you do need to understand how marketing teams work together so you can keep everyone aligned.



WHY YOU SHOULD APPLY



This is a leadership role at a growing company. You're not joining a huge corporation where nobody knows your name. You'll work directly with the founding partners and have real influence over how things are done.



As we add more clients, this role grows with us. The person we hire now will become a key leader on the team.



Fixed monthly rate plus performance bonus based on client results. When the team delivers, you benefit.



Fully remote. Work from anywhere.



You'll learn a lot. If you want to grow your skills in marketing operations and team management, this role will push you in a good way.



TOOLS YOU'LL USE



GoHighLevel, Zapier/Make, Notion, Google Workspace, Zoom (training provided if needed)



HOW TO APPLY

We are scheduling interviews this Friday. To be considered, you MUST submit your application through our application form here:





Your application MUST include a Loom introduction video. In your Loom (1-5 minutes), cover:



Introduce yourself and tell us about your experience managing or coordinating a team — how many people, what type of work, what tools you used

Give us an example of a time you kept a project or launch on track when things got complicated. What was the situation and what did you do?

Tell us why this role interests you and what makes you a good fit



We want to get to know you. Applications without a Loom video will not be reviewed.

Do NOT apply through OnlineJobs.ph messaging. Only applications submitted through the form link above will be considered.

 in a professional and timely manner

Photo editing



Video editing



Social media management



SEO fundamentals



Google Docs / Microsoft Word

Requirements

Willingness to learn and improve

title: Senior Application Security Engineer
description:
Senior Application Security Engineer - Course & Exercise Creator

Who We Are

At Practical DevSecOps, we create hands-on security training that actually works. Our courses

have trained thousands of professionals worldwide in DevSecOps, API Security, Threat

Modeling, and AI Security. We're looking for someone who loves researching vulnerabilities,

designing creative exercises, and writing content that makes complex security concepts click for

learners.

What You'll Actually Be Doing

-Create Amazing Training Content

-Design and build brand new security exercises that demonstrate real-world vulnerabilities

-Research emerging security trends and translate them into practical, hands-on labs

-Write clear, engaging exercise instructions that guide learners step-by-step

-Develop entirely new courses in emerging security domains (AI Security, Cloud Native

Security, etc.)

-Create documentation, guides, and reference materials that learners will actually use

-Build vulnerable applications and scenarios using our open source projects

Research & Innovation

-Stay curious - constantly explore new security tools, techniques, and vulnerabilities

-Experiment with different approaches to teaching complex security concepts

-Test and validate new security tools to see if they're worth including in our training

-Read security research papers and figure out how to turn them into practical exercises

-Contribute innovative ideas to our open source security projects

-Maintain & Improve Existing Content

-Fix exercises when tools get updated (things break, it happens)

-Modernize labs with newer versions of tools and technologies

-Improve exercises based on student feedback and instructor insights

-Troubleshoot technical issues in training environments

What You Need to Have

Must have:



-Love for writing - you enjoy creating technical content and explaining complex things

simply

-Natural curiosity - you're the type who sees a new security tool and immediately wants to

test it

-Hands-on application security experience - you've found and exploited vulnerabilities

-Strong programming skills (Python, Java, JavaScript) - you can build vulnerable apps

from scratch

-Experience with security tools: SAST/DAST scanners, OWASP ZAP, Burp Suite,

SonarQube, Snyk, etc.

-DevSecOps practical knowledge - CI/CD pipelines, containers, cloud security

-Understanding of OWASP Top 10 and common vulnerability patterns

-Self-motivated researcher who doesn't need hand-holding

Nice to Have:

-You've written technical blog posts, articles, or documentation before

-Created training materials, labs, or exercises (in any domain)

-Contributed to open source projects

-Security certifications (CEH, OSCP, or others)

-Experience teaching or presenting technical topics

-Taken our courses or similar hands-on security training

About You

You're someone who:

-Gets genuinely excited about discovering new vulnerabilities and security techniques

-Enjoys the challenge of explaining technical concepts in simple terms

-Loves spending time researching and experimenting with new tools

-Takes pride in creating content that helps others learn

-Can work independently and manage your own research and content creation timeline

-Doesn't mind the iterative process of writing, testing, getting feedback, and improving

Why This Role is Special

Your exercises will be used by thousands of security professionals worldwide

You'll have creative freedom to research and build what you think is important

You'll shape the future of practical security education

Work with cutting-edge security tools and techniques daily

Professional development budget for certifications, conferences, and tools



Join a passionate team that values education and innovation

title: Marketing Assistant (Full-Time, WFH – Day Shift)
description:
Ready to Execute with Excellence? We’re Hiring a Marketing Assistant Who Gets It Done



Are you the kind of person who sees what needs to happen, and just makes it happen?



We’re Living Well Psychology & Support, a values-driven mental health business on a mission to help people define what “living well” really means. We practice what we preach, and we’re looking for a marketing-focused VA who’s ready to roll up their sleeves and bring our strategy to life as we grow.



This is your chance to implement powerful marketing campaigns, streamline our content and communications, and help expand our impact.



Why This Role Rocks: 

- You’ll work closely with a neurodiverse founder who values structure, clarity, and results 

- Your work will directly drive our growth: ads launched, leads captured, lives changed 

- You’ll own the marketing metrics and be responsible for reporting and results 

- No micromanagement, just ownership, trust, and impact



What You’ll Do: 

- Design, publish, and monitor paid ad campaigns (Google/FB/IG) 

- Manage our GHL funnels,
 marketing, landing pages, and website content 

- Build marketing assets (e.g., lead magnets, blog posts, newsletters, visuals) 

- Track performance data, report on metrics, and optimise campaigns



How We’ll Measure Success: 

- Leads and conversions from ads and funnels 

- Campaigns launched and optimised on schedule 

- Content calendar delivered consistently 

- Clear, consistent reporting on marketing performance



You’re Perfect For This If You: 

- Are obsessed with clarity, detail, and execution 

- Have experience with Canva, GHL, WordPress, Meta Suite 

- Love bringing structure to marketing processes and owning results 

- Enjoy working in a fast-moving, purpose-driven team



Salary & Perks: 

- Salary: ?45,000–?60,000/month (AUD $1,200–$1,600) 

- Bonus: Up to 10% quarterly based on performance 

- Perks: Flexible hours, training budget, real growth path as the business scales



How to Apply: Send your CV and a 2–3 minute Loom video answering: 

- What excites you about this marketing role? 

- Share a time you launched or managed a campaign and what results it drove 

- What tools or systems do you rely on to stay organised and consistent?



Deadline: Submit by October 28. Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted. 



Let’s grow together!

title: SEO Specialist (Australian Businesses – Ongoing Work)
description:
We are currently looking for an experienced and reliable SEO Specialist to join our team and assist with ongoing SEO campaigns for clients across Australia.



Our agency works primarily with trades and service-based businesses such as electricians, plumbers, builders, landscapers, and other local service providers. Because of this, we are specifically looking for someone who has previous experience working with Australian businesses, understands local SEO, and knows how to generate real, measurable results.



This role will involve both strategy and execution, with a strong focus on local SEO performance, content, and ongoing optimisation. There is consistent ongoing work available, and we are ideally looking for someone who can become a long-term part of our team.



Responsibilities:



• Conduct SEO audits and identify opportunities for improvement

• Perform keyword research focused on local Australian search intent

• Implement on-page SEO optimisations (meta titles, descriptions, headings, internal linking, etc.)

• Write and publish SEO-optimised blog content 

• Assist with Google Business Profile (GBP) management including posts, updates, and optimisation (training can be provided)

• Build local citations and directory listings to improve authority and NAP consistency

• Support location page creation and expansion targeting specific suburbs and service areas

• Carry out basic technical SEO checks (Search Console, errors, page speed, etc.)

• Provide monthly reporting including rankings, traffic insights, and work completed

• Work alongside our team to continuously improve campaign performance



Requirements:



• Proven experience in SEO

• Experience working with Australian businesses (preferably trades or service-based industries)

• Strong understanding of local SEO and Google rankings

• Ability to write or assist with SEO content (blogs, service pages, etc.)

• Good written and spoken English communication skills

• Experience with tools such as Google Search Console, Google Analytics, and SEO platforms

• Strong attention to detail and ability to follow structured processes

• Ability to show proof of results (rankings, traffic growth, lead generation, etc.)



To confirm that you have read the full job listing, please include the phrase “I listen to Dylan” somewhere in your application.



When applying, please include examples of SEO work you have completed, particularly for Australian businesses, along with any results, case studies, or data you can share.



We are looking for someone who can become a reliable and results-driven part of our team, helping us grow rankings, traffic, and leads for our clients through consistent and effective SEO work.

title: Dental Billing Specialist {$5-$8/HR}
description:
Dental Billing Specialist  (USD $5–$8/hr)



Join a modern, tech-forward dental office that values accuracy, kindness, and a great patient experience.



We are a modern dental practice based in Las Vegas, Nevada, and we’re looking for an experienced Dental Billing  Specialist to join our team and help us deliver an exceptional patient experience.



This is a long-term, full-time remote position. We’re looking for someone who is organized, detail-oriented, and highly familiar with U.S. billing process—especially candidates who have worked with outsourced dental billing/eligibility companies.



Ideal Background (Highly Preferred)

-Experience working for or with companies such as REACH, eAssist, or other outsourced dental billing/insurance verification teams

-Strong understanding of U.S. dental insurance workflows and real-world plan nuances



Responsibilities & Requirements:

-Minimum 2 years of experience in Dental Billing and claims tracing/resubmissions (U.S. dental insurance) 

-Must have experience using Open Dental

-Confident navigating dental insurance portals/websites for claims and pre-auth status 

-Confident resubmitting claims/pre-authorization via online portals/websites 

-Confident identifying Teeth #’s and able to label xrays (quadrants, pre-op, post-op xray)

-Able to call insurance companies for claims status after multiple resubmissions 

-Able to call insurance companies for appeal status 

-Understands the difference between clearing house and online resubmissions 

-Can enter claim payments after payment clearance

-Can enter pre-authorization in Open Dental  

-Able to clearly document narratives and claims status

-Able to assistant with miscellaneous Billing Task as needed 



Work Requirements:

-Reliable internet connection

-Quiet, professional home workspace

-Availability during Pacific Standard Time (8:00 AM – 4:00 PM)

-Excellent English communication skills (spoken and written)

-Comfortable working inside Open Dental daily

-Must be able to receive payments via Payoneer



What We Offer:

-Competitive pay based on experience (with annual raises)

-Long-term, full-time remote position

-Supportive team culture built on kindness, growth, and professionalism

-Training provided on our specific systems and workflow



To Apply:

Please send the following:

-Your resume

-Respond to our ad with the number 20

-A short voice recording introducing yourself and describing your experience working with Dental Billing and claims tracing (Please include any experience with REACH, eAssist, or similar outsourcing/billing/verification companies.)



We’re excited to hear from you and potentially welcome you to our team!

title: General Admin & Finance
description:
## About Zelix Labs



Zelix Labs is a Singapore-based digital growth agency that helps SMBs across Southeast Asia grow through AI-powered marketing automation, WhatsApp business messaging, and paid advertising. As we grow, we need someone to keep the operational and financial side of the business running smoothly so the founders and delivery team can focus on clients.



---



## The Role



You will handle the day-to-day administrative and financial operations of Zelix Labs. This includes invoicing, expense tracking, contractor payments, document management, and general operational support. You will be the person who makes sure nothing falls through the cracks on the business operations side.



This is a foundational role — not glamorous, but essential. The right person is organised, reliable, detail-oriented, and takes pride in keeping things running smoothly.



---



## What You Will Do



**Finance (approximately 50% of time):**



- Prepare and send client invoices (monthly retainers, one-time project fees)

- Track payments received and follow up on overdue invoices

- Record expenses and maintain financial records in Google Sheets or accounting software

- Process contractor payments — track hours/deliverables and prepare payment summaries

- Reconcile ad spend billing (Meta, Google, TikTok) against client invoices to ensure accuracy

- Prepare monthly financial summaries for the founders — revenue, expenses, outstanding payments, cash position

- Support annual tax preparation and filing by maintaining organised records

- Manage receipts and documentation for expense claims



**Admin (approximately 50% of time):**



- Maintain the company's document library — contracts, proposals, SOPs, client files

- Schedule meetings and manage the founders' calendars when needed

- Coordinate onboarding logistics for new tea
mbers — access setup, tool accounts, documentation

- Manage software subscriptions and renewals — track what we pay for, when renewals are due, and flag unused tools

- Handle correspondence — respond to routine
s, forward client enquiries to the right tea
mber

- Maintain the internal team directory and contact information

- Assist with ad-hoc operational tasks — research, data entry, report formatting

- Help maintain CRM records and client data hygiene



---



## What We Are Looking For



**Must have:**



- 2+ years of experience in administrative support, bookkeeping, virtual assistant, or office management

- Strong organisational skills — you keep track of deadlines, follow up on pending items, and do not let things slip

- Attention to detail — especially with numbers. Invoice errors erode client trust.

- Proficiency with Google Workspace (Sheets, Docs, Drive, Calendar)

- Basic bookkeeping/accounting knowledge — you understand invoices, expenses, profit and loss, and cash flow at a fundamental level

- Good written English for professional correspondence

- Trustworthy and discreet — you will handle financial information and contracts

- Self-directed — you do not need to be told to follow up on an overdue invoice or renew an expiring subscription



**Nice to have:**



- Experience with accounting software (Xero, QuickBooks, Wave, or similar)

- Experience with Singapore business administration (IRAS, ACRA basics)

- Experience in a digital marketing agency or tech company

- Familiarity with project management tools (Notion, Asana, Trello, or similar)

- Experience managing contractor relationships and payments across multiple currencies (SGD, PHP, USD)

- Basic understanding of SaaS billing models and subscription management



---



## What We Provide



- Premium AI tools subscription — useful for drafting correspondence, formatting documents, and summarising financial data

- Clear processes and templates — we are building SOPs for everything and you will help maintain them

- A growing company where you will be the first dedicated admin/finance person — significant ownership of the function

- Direct access to the founders

- Stable, long-term role — this need does not go away as we grow; it gets more important



---



## Compensation



?25,000 – ?37,500/month (approximately SGD 530 – 800) depending on experience.



Higher range for candidates with bookkeeping skills or Singapore business admin experience.



---



## How to Apply



Please include:



1. A short Loom video (3–5 minutes) walking us through how you keep finances and admin organised in your current or most recent role — show your spreadsheets, tools, or systems if possible. This is the single most important part of your application.

2. Your OnlineJobs.ph profile link

3. A brief summary of your admin/finance experience — what you managed, the size of the business, and the tools you used

4. Your proficiency level with spreadsheets (basic, intermediate, advanced) — be honest

5. Whether you have experience with invoicing, bookkeeping, or financial record-keeping

6. Your availability to start

title: Order Fulfillment Specialist (SPS Commerce / EDI)
description:
Are you experienced in managing order fulfillment, retailer compliance, and 3PL coordination?



We’re looking for a detail-oriented Order Fulfillment Specialist who can manage order flow end-to-end, ensure accuracy, and proactively resolve issues before they escalate.



What You’ll Do

• Manage order uploads via SPS Commerce and retailer portals

• Monitor open orders and ensure on-time fulfillment

• Review daily reports and flag discrepancies or risks

• Coordinate with 3PL partners to ensure smooth operations

• Handle retailer disputes, invoicing, and compliance requirements

• Track orders end-to-end and resolve delays proactively

• Maintain accurate records and reporting



What We’re Looking For

• 1–2+ years in fulfillment, logistics, 3PL, or retail operations

• Hands-on experience with SPS Commerce / EDI systems

• Strong understanding of order pipelines and fulfillment workflows

• Experience with retailer compliance, disputes, and invoicing

• Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple deadlines

• Clear and professional communication skills

• Proficient in Excel or Google Sheets



Nice to Have

• Experience with Cin7 or similar systems

• Familiarity with vendor routing guides

• Experience working with 3PL or warehouse teams



Please answer the screening questions below. Failure to do so will affect your application. 

1. Can you walk me through your experience managing purchase orders or e-commerce orders? What systems or platforms did you use to process and track them?



2. Have you worked with retailer portals or EDI platforms such as SPS Commerce or similar systems? If yes, what tasks were you responsible for within those platforms?



3. How did you typically monitor open orders to ensure they shipped on time? What steps did you take if you noticed an order might miss its shipping deadline?



4. Have you worked directly with warehouses or third-party logistics (3PL) partners? What did your communication and coordination with them usually involve?



5. Tell us about a time an order or shipment issue occurred (e.g., inventory shortage, shipment delay, incorrect quantities). How did you identify the problem and resolve it?



6. What types of operational reports or tracking did you regularly maintain (e.g., order reports, inventory tracking, shipment status)?

title: Web Designer
description:
## About Zelix Labs



Zelix Labs is a Singapore-based digital growth agency that helps SMBs across Southeast Asia grow through AI-powered marketing automation, WhatsApp business messaging, and paid advertising. We build high-converting websites and landing pages for our clients as part of our growth service packages.



Our web builds are powered by a unique workflow: we use advanced AI tools with custom design systems and web standards that we have developed in-house. This means you will be producing websites faster and at higher quality than most agencies — but you still need strong design sense and coding ability to guide the AI and refine the output.



---



## The Role



You will build and maintain client websites and landing pages — primarily single-page marketing sites, multi-page business sites, and high-converting ad landing pages. Most builds are HTML/CSS/JS deployed on Cloudflare Pages.



You will work with our internal design system and AI-powered skills to generate initial builds, then refine them with your own design judgement and coding skills. This is not a template-dragging role — we build custom, high-quality sites that look and perform like premium agency work.



---



## What You Will Do



- Build client websites and landing pages from briefs — typically 1-page to 5-page sites for Singapore SMBs

- Use AI tools with our custom web standards and design system to generate initial code, then refine and polish the output

- Ensure all builds meet our standards: mobile-first responsive design, Core Web Vitals performance (LCP, CLS, INP), SEO best practices, proper meta tags, and schema markup

- Implement WhatsApp click-to-chat buttons, sticky CTAs, contact forms, and conversion tracking

- Maintain and update existing client sites — content changes, design tweaks, new sections

- Collaborate with the Media Buyer to ensure landing pages align with ad campaigns (messaging match, conversion tracking)

- Deploy sites to Cloudflare Pages and manage DNS/hosting configurations

- QA all builds across devices (iPhone Safari, Android Chrome, desktop) before client review

- Follow and contribute to our internal web standards documentation



---



## What We Are Looking For



**Must have:**



- 2+ years of experience building marketing websites or landing pages

- Strong HTML, CSS, and JavaScript skills — you can hand-code a responsive page from scratch

- Good visual design sense — you understand typography, spacing, colour, and layout principles

- Experience with mobile-first responsive design

- Understanding of page speed optimisation (image compression, lazy loading, font loading, minimal render-blocking resources)

- Comfortable with Git or similar version control

- Portfolio showing at least 5 sites or landing pages you have built (links or screenshots)

- Good written English for communication with the team



**Nice to have:**



- Experience with Cloudflare Pages, Netlify, or Vercel for deployment

- Understanding of SEO fundamentals (meta tags, heading structure, schema markup, OG tags)

- Experience with Google Tag Manager and conversion tracking implementation

- Familiarity with AI tools (ChatGPT, Cursor, or similar) for code generation

- Experience with animation libraries or CSS animations (scroll-triggered reveals, micro-interactions)

- Experience building sites for Singapore or Southeast Asian businesses

- Basic understanding of Figma or design tools for reviewing design references



---



## What We Provide



- Premium AI tools subscription with access to our custom design system, web standards, animated components library, SEO/AEO skill, and performance optimisation skill — these dramatically accelerate your workflow

- Clear web standards documentation covering typography, spacing, animations, copywriting frameworks, and mobile requirements

- Design pattern library with five pre-built patterns suited to different verticals and client types

- Cloudflare Pages deployment pipeline — no complex hosting setup to manage

- Training on our workflow and standards from the founders

- Autonomy — once you understand our standards, you will own the full build process



---



## Compensation



?33,000 – ?56,000/month (approximately SGD 700 – 1,200) depending on experience.



This is a long-term role. As the client base grows, there will be opportunity to take on more complex projects and potentially lead a small web team.



---



## How to Apply



Please include:



1. A short Loom video (3–5 minutes) walking us through one website or landing page you built — show the live site, explain your design decisions, how you handled mobile responsiveness, and any performance optimisations you made. This is the single most important part of your application.

2. Your OnlineJobs.ph profile link

3. Links to 3–5 websites or landing pages you have built (live links preferred, screenshots accepted)

4. A brief description of your typical build process — tools you use, how long a typical landing page takes you, and how you handle mobile responsiveness

5. Your experience with any AI coding tools

6. Your availability to start

title: Marketing Executive
description:
## About Zelix Labs



Zelix Labs is a Singapore-based digital growth agency that helps SMBs across Southeast Asia grow through AI-powered marketing automation, WhatsApp business messaging, and paid advertising. We are building a new kind of agency — one where AI tools amplify every tea
mber's output.



We are looking for a Marketing Executive who can help us grow our own brand while supporting client marketing initiatives.



---



## The Role



This is a hybrid role. You will split your time between growing Zelix Labs' own brand presence (content, social media, case studies, lead generation) and supporting client marketing campaigns (content creation, campaign coordination, copywriting).



You will work closely with the founders, the Media Buyer, and the Ad Creative Designer to produce marketing content and coordinate campaigns across multiple channels.



---



## What You Will Do



**Zelix Labs brand (approximately 40% of time):**



- Create and manage content for Zelix Labs' social media channels (LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok)

- Write case studies and client success stories based on real campaign data

- Help produce thought leadership content about AI in marketing, WhatsApp business automation, and SMB growth

- Support lead generation campaigns — manage ad copy, landing page content, and
 sequences

- Maintain and update the Zelix Labs website content



**Client marketing support (approximately 60% of time):**



- Write ad copy for client campaigns across Meta, Google, and TikTok (working alongside AI tools)

- Create content calendars and WhatsApp campaign schedules for clients

- Coordinate with the Ad Creative Designer on visual assets for campaigns

- Write website copy for client landing pages and site builds

- Help prepare client proposals and strategy documents using our internal templates

- Research competitors, market trends, and seasonal opportunities for client verticals



---



## What We Are Looking For



**Must have:**



- 2+ years of experience in digital marketing, content marketing, or a similar role

- Strong copywriting skills — you can write ad copy, social media posts, website copy, and longer-form content (case studies, articles)

- Understanding of social media platforms and content best practices

- Comfortable working with data — you can read a campaign report and turn the key findings into a compelling story

- Good research skills — you can quickly learn about a new industry or vertical and write credibly about it

- Excellent written English — this is a content-heavy role

- Organised and able to manage multiple projects with different deadlines



**Nice to have:**



- Experience writing for Singapore or Southeast Asian audiences

- Experience with AI writing tools (ChatGPT or similar) and willingness to use them as part of your workflow

- Basic understanding of paid advertising (Meta Ads, Google Ads)

- Experience with
 marketing tools (Mailchimp, Brevo, or similar)

- Experience creating content for B2B services or professional services

- Basic design skills (Canva, Figma) for creating simple social media visuals

- Knowledge of SEO and content optimisation



---



## What We Provide



- Premium AI tools subscription for AI-assisted copywriting, research, and content creation

- Internal templates for proposals, case studies, and campaign plans

- Exposure to multiple verticals and industries — dental, automotive, beauty, home services, F&B, and more

- Direct mentorship from the founders

- A growing company where you can shape the marketing function from early stage



---



## Compensation



?25,000 – ?47,000/month (approximately SGD 530 – 1,000) depending on experience.



This role has strong growth potential. As Zelix Labs scales, this could evolve into a Marketing Manager or Content Lead position.



---



## How to Apply



Please include:



1. A short Loom video (3–5 minutes) walking us through a piece of marketing content you created — an ad campaign, a social media strategy, or a case study. Explain the goal, your approach, and the outcome. This is the single most important part of your application.

2. Your OnlineJobs.ph profile link

3. 2–3 writing samples — ideally a mix of short-form (ad copy or social post) and long-form (article, case study, or blog post)

4. Links to any social media accounts or brands you have managed

5. A brief note on how you would approach building a social media presence for a B2B agency from scratch

6. Your availability to start

title: Student Enrollment & Sales Advisor
description:
About Otips


 helps international students already in the U.S. navigate school placement, change of status, Day 1 CPT, F-1 transfers, housing, medical insurance, and other essential student services. We've helped 800+ students from 15+ countries and we're growing fast.

This is a mission-driven company built by someone who went through the international student experience firsthand — and knew it could be done better.



About the Role

We're looking for a smart, proactive, and sales-minded Student Enrollment & Sales Advisor who can connect with students, understand their situation, explain our services clearly, follow up consistently, and help convert leads into paying clients.

This is not a passive customer service role. You'll be part advisor, part sales consultant, part enrollment coordinator — someone who takes ownership and gets things done.



What You'll Do



Reply to leads and students through WhatsApp,
, CRM, and social media

Talk to prospective students, understand their situation and goals, and recommend the right Otips service

Explain our services clearly and help students feel confident moving forward

Follow up with leads consistently and help close sales

Collect documents and guide students through the enrollment process

Coordinate with internal tea
mbers and partner schools

Keep CRM records accurate and up to date

Support students before, during, and after enrollment

Escalate legal or complex immigration questions to the right tea
mber





What We're Looking For



Strong spoken and written English

Experience in customer service, sales, admissions, enrollment, or student advising

Confident communicator who builds trust quickly

Someone who can consult and sell — not just answer questions

Strong follow-up habits and attention to detail

Comfortable managing multiple leads and cases simultaneously

Able to work independently and take ownership without being micromanaged

Friendly, professional, and dependable

Experience with international students, U.S.-based clients, or education is a big plus

Familiarity with CRM tools, Google Sheets, WhatsApp, and remote work preferred





Compensation



Base salary: $800–$1,200/month USD, depending on experience

Performance incentives based on conversions and enrollments

Room to grow as the company grows





Work Schedule



Full-time

Must be available Monday–Friday, with at least 4 hours overlap with Eastern Time (ET) business hours (9 AM–6 PM ET)

Some flexibility for evening or weekend follow-up may be needed





Why Join Us



Work directly with the founder of a fast-growing company

Fully remote role

We've helped 800+ students from 15+ countries — this work has real impact

Performance incentives on top of base salary

Long-term growth opportunity as we scale





To Apply, Please Send:



Your resume

A short introduction about yourself

Your relevant experience in customer service, sales, enrollment, or advising

Your expected monthly salary

Your availability and time zone (ET overlap required)

A 1–2 minute voice or video introduction in English

Answer this question: A student in the U.S. is interested in changing their status to F-1 and wants to know how Otips can help. How would you respond?



Applications without the voice/video intro and scenario answer will not be reviewed.

title: Remote Medical Front Desk
description:
JOB TITLE: Medical Front Desk (U.S. Healthcare Hawaii)



We’re a U.S. medical clinic hiring a long-term remote VA for front desk + scheduling. Work hours will be from 730AM HST to 1200 PM HST and from 130PM HST to 430PM HST



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

- Answer/return patient calls, schedule/reschedule, confirm appointments

- Insurance verification, basic benefits questions, collect required info

- Document clearly inside the EHR and keep tasks organized



REQUIREMENTS (BUT FLEXIBLE)

- 1+ year U.S. healthcare front desk / scheduling / patient access experience (remote or in-clinic)

- Strong spoken + written English (natural, easy for U.S. patients to understand)

- Comfortable with: insurance verification, referrals, copays/deductibles

- Reliable internet, quiet workspace, headset; HIPAA-minded work habits

- NO agencies/teams. You must be the person doing the work (no outsourcing).



HOW TO APPLY (FOLLOW EXACTLY OR DISQUALIFIED)

Start your message with: AVC



Include ALL of the following:

1) Resume/CV link (PDF via Google Drive/Dropbox; set to “anyone with link can view”)

2) Answer these (real examples only; 4–8 sentences each):

- Q1: A prior auth was denied and the patient was upset. What did you do step-by-step?

- Q2: You caught a scheduling/insurance mistake before the visit. How did you fix it?

3) Loom video link (60–90 sec, camera ON):

- State your U.S. healthcare role + years

- Say this line naturally: “Thank you for calling our clinic—this is [Name]. May I have your insurance company and member ID so I can verify benefits before we schedule?”



Missing resume link, missing Loom, audio-only video, agency/team applications, or generic/AI-style answers = disqualified.

title: Job Title: SEO Specialist for Shopify Furniture Store (On-Page, Off-Page & Technical SEO)
description:

**Website:**

**About Us**



SoftSeason Furniture is an e-commerce furniture brand based in Canada. We sell high-quality dining sets, massage chairs, and modern furniture. Our website and SEO foundations are already set up, and we are now looking for an experienced **Senior SEO Specialist** to help audit, optimize, and scale our organic traffic.

---

* Perform a full technical SEO audit

* Improve page speed and Core Web Vitals

* Strong communication and reporting skills



---

---

To confirm you read this job post, please start your application with the word **SoftSeason**.

title: Senior Magento 2 Full-Stack Developer (Microservices Focus)

Ensure system reliability, scalability, and maintainability

Requirements



5+ years experience as a Magento 2 Full-Stack Developer

Proven expertise in PHP, JavaScript, HTML, CSS

Strong experience with Magento Enterprise (Adobe Commerce)

Demonstrated experience in system architecture and microservices design

Experience building and consuming REST/GraphQL APIs

Experience working with large and complex product catalogs

Nice to Have

What We Offer



100% Remote Work

Full-time position

Work on complex, high-scale e-commerce systems

Ideal Candidate



You are not just a developer—you are a problem solver and system thinker who can:



Work within Magento today

Design what comes next

Execute a clean transition to a scalable microservices ecosystem

title: Sourcing Assistant - AU Hours
description:
Sourcing Assistant (Contractor, AU Hours)

Location: Remote (positions available for Sydney time zone)

Engagement Type: Full-time Contractor

Initial Review Period: 3 months

Reports to: Sourcing Manager



About the Company

We are a leading buyer's agent in Australia, specialising in investment properties across metropolitan and regional areas. Our team is dedicated to identifying and securing high-potential properties for our clients, backed by thorough research, detailed analysis and expert insights.



Role Overview

The Sourcing Assistant is an important part of our team, responsible for assisting in the initial due diligence and tracking of property deals to ensure they meet our standards. You will also handle ad hoc administrative and research tasks that support smooth sourcing operations.



This role involves regular communication with solicitors, conveyancers and sales agents, so strong English communication skills, both written and verbal, are essential. You will need excellent attention to detail, the ability to work independently, and a strong working relationship with the Sourcing Manager. It’s a fast-paced role that suits someone organised, proactive and adaptable.



Key Responsibilities

- Complete initial due diligence for incoming property deals as directed by the Sourcing Manager

- Track and update the progress of property deals from sourcing through to contract stage

- Prepare detailed summaries including property and agent comments, statistical analysis, quotes and risk assessments (such as flood or fire maps)

- Communicate professionally with solicitors, conveyancers and sales agents to confirm deal details, request information and support transactions

- Compile relevant documentation such as property comparisons and rental appraisal letters, maintaining organised records in Google Drive

- Assist with ad hoc administrative and research tasks to support sourcing and wider team operations

- Transition to assistant-level interactions with property agents after gaining experience

- Work closely with the Sourcing Manager to ensure clear communication and alignment on property evaluations



Qualifications and Experience

- Experience in real estate or property-related work is preferred but not essential

- Familiarity with Australian property tools (for example RP Data or CoreLogic) is highly regarded

- Strong administrative and organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities



Skills

- Excellent written and verbal English communication

- Exceptional attention to detail

- Strong analytical and problem-solving ability

- Ability to work independently and deliver work efficiently

- Comfortable working closely with the Sourcing Manager in a one-on-one setting



Work Environment

- This is a remote role that requires a reliable internet connection during Australian business hours. 

- This is a contractor role, not an employee position. You will be engaged under a service agreement and responsible for your own tax and insurance obligations.



Career Progression

You’ll receive one-on-one training and ongoing support, with opportunities to grow within the company.

title: Ad Creative Owner / Direct Response Creative Producer
description:
Machete Muay Thai is looking for a hands-on Ad Creative Owner to take responsibility for our paid social creative from idea to final ad.



This is not a role for someone who only gives strategy, writes a few hooks, or sends briefs for someone else to figure out.



We want one person who can own the creative process end to end and help us produce ads that actually perform.



What this role is

You will be responsible for turning our offers, customer avatar, gym footage, testimonials, and brand message into direct-response ads built to convert.



That means you can:

come up with angles and concepts

write hooks, scripts, copy, and CTAs

create shot lists and filming direction

work with our team on what needs to be filmed

review raw footage

give clear edit feedback

help shape the final ad

create static ad concepts

iterate based on what is working

Editing ability is a bonus, but not essential.

This role is NOT for you if:

you only do strategy

you only write copy

you only edit

you only make things look good without understanding conversion

you need someone else to translate your ideas into a usable creative process

you have no proof of direct-response work

you cannot show finished ads you personally helped lead from concept to final output



Pay

Pay is negotiable depending on skill level and proof of work.



What we need help with

We run paid ads for beginner offers at Machete Muay Thai in Penrith, NSW.

Our ideal creative operator understands how to make ads for people who:

feel unfit

lack structure

are sick of normal gyms

want something that actually challenges them

are interested in Muay Thai but may feel intimidated starting

You must be able to create ads that feel:

direct

sharp

emotionally accurate

beginner-friendly without being soft

performance-focused, not generic fitness fluff



Responsibilities

Own the ad creative pipeline from concept to final output

Develop fresh angles, hooks, and ad concepts

Write primary text, headlines, scripts, and CTA variations

Build clear briefs and shot lists for our team

Direct creative execution remotely

Review footage and provide edit feedback

Help improve creative based on results

Work collaboratively with our internal team to keep quality high and output consistent

Requirements

Proven direct-response experience for Meta ads

Strong portfolio of ads you personally led

Ability to explain your thinking clearly

Strong copywriting and creative strategy skills

Good understanding of hooks, personas, objections, and offer positioning

Ability to take ownership without being spoon-fed

Strong communication and turnaround time

Comfortable working with a premium combat sports / fitness brand

To apply

Do not send a generic application.



Please send:

A short intro video about who you are and what you do

3–5 examples of finished ads you personally led from concept to final output

A breakdown of what parts of the process you personally owned on each ad

Any results you can share

Your rate and availability

A short Loom video walking through one ad and explaining:

the angle

the hook

why it worked

what you personally owned in the process



Application task

Using this avatar:

Beginner male in Penrith who feels unfit, lacks structure, is sick of normal gyms, and wants something that actually challenges him



Create:

3 hooks

2 ad angles

1 short script

1 image ad concept

1 video ad concept

1 simple shot list / creative direction for how it should be filmed

Important



We are not looking for:

an agency

a pure strategist

a pure editor

a content creator who does not understand direct response



We are looking for someone who can own the process, improve the creative, and help us make ads that convert.

If that sounds like you, hit apply.
